title: Move An AGV To Another Map Location Using The Go-to Function
summary: Use the Go-to function in the robot selection interface to send an AGV that is not currently in a task to a selected square or map cell, avoiding the need to physically push the AGV.
when_to_use: Use when an AGV is not currently in a task and needs to be repositioned to another map location. The source describes a green AGV state as ready to execute something but not currently having a task, and identifies the Go-to function as useful for quick recovery and repositioning.
not_for: Do not use when the AGV is currently in a task.

Do not use this procedure as a complete recovery workflow for cases where the AGV still does not pick up a new task after being moved, because the source does not provide further operator decision logic beyond the example described.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the AGV, choose the Go-to function, select the destination square or target code, and confirm the move.

safety_notes: Use only when the AGV is not in a task, as stated by the source.
The source presents this function as a way to avoid physically pushing the AGV.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the AGV control interface with robot selection
Map or target code destination selection

access_required: Access to the robot selection area in the AGV control interface
system_preconditions: The AGV is not currently in a task.
The AGV is in a green state indicating it is ready to execute something but does not currently have a task.

visual_references.description: Training slide and transcript segment showing the Robot Selection Go-to function workflow and describing use for an AGV not in a task.

steps.title: Verify the AGV is idle and eligible for Go-to
steps.purpose: Ensure the procedure is only used on an AGV that is ready but not currently assigned a task.
steps.instruction: Identify an AGV that is not in a task. Confirm the AGV is in the green state described by the source as ready to execute something but not currently having a task.
steps.expected_result: An eligible AGV is identified for repositioning.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is green and not currently in a task.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV is in a task or its state does not match the source-described ready/no-task condition.

actions.label: Check AGV status
actions.exact_action: Identify an AGV that is not in a task; the source states a green state means the AGV is ready to execute something but does not currently have a task.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection Go-to function slide and transcript note describing green state and no current task.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the AGV is currently in a task.
Stop if the AGV state does not indicate the ready/no-task condition described by the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If an AGV is in the green state, that means it is ready to execute something, but it does not have a task.
Green indicates the AGV is ready to execute but currently has no task.

steps.title: Select the AGV in robot selection
steps.purpose: Choose the specific AGV that will be moved.
steps.instruction: Select the AGV in the robot selection area.
steps.expected_result: The intended AGV is selected in the interface.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV is visibly selected in robot selection.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV is not selected or the wrong AGV is selected.

actions.label: Select AGV
actions.exact_action: Select the AGV in the robot selection area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot selection area showing the AGV selection step.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if you cannot identify or select the intended AGV.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide OCR lists steps: select an AGV, select Go to, select which square to send the AGV to, and select Confirm.

steps.title: Select the Go to function
steps.purpose: Open the movement function used to send the selected AGV to a chosen location.
steps.instruction: Select the Go to function for the selected AGV.
steps.expected_result: The interface is ready for destination selection.
steps.healthy_condition: The Go to function is active for the selected AGV.
steps.failure_condition: The Go to function is unavailable or not applied to the selected AGV.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Go to function or button in the Robot Selection workflow.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Go to function cannot be selected for the AGV.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The transcript explains that if an AGV is in a green state and ready but has no task, the operator can select the AGV, choose Go to, select a target code or click a location on the map, and confirm.

steps.title: Choose the destination location
steps.purpose: Specify where the AGV should be sent on the map.
steps.instruction: Choose the destination by either entering the target code or clicking a square or cell on the map.
steps.expected_result: A destination square, cell, or target code is selected for the AGV.
steps.healthy_condition: The intended destination is selected in the interface.
steps.failure_condition: No destination is selected or the wrong destination is selected.

actions.label: Select destination
actions.exact_action: Choose the destination by either entering the target code or clicking a square or cell on the map.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map or interface showing selectable destination squares or cells for the Go to action.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the destination cannot be selected.
Stop if you are not confident the selected square or cell is the intended target.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The operator can select a target code or click a location on the map.
Select which square to send the AGV to.

steps.title: Confirm the move
steps.purpose: Send the selected AGV to the chosen location.
steps.instruction: Select Confirm to send the AGV to the chosen location.
steps.expected_result: The AGV is dispatched to the selected target location.
steps.healthy_condition: The AGV begins navigating to the selected location without needing to be physically pushed.
steps.failure_condition: The AGV does not begin moving to the selected location after confirmation.

actions.label: Confirm destination
actions.exact_action: Select Confirm to send the AGV to the chosen location.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Confirm action after choosing the destination.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the AGV does not navigate to the selected target location after confirmation.
Escalate if the AGV does not pick up a task after being moved and additional decision logic is needed, because the source does not provide further operator guidance.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The slide OCR lists steps: select an AGV, select Go to, select which square to send the AGV to, and select Confirm.

success_criteria: The intended AGV is selected and a destination square, cell, or target code is chosen.
The operator confirms the Go-to action.
The AGV navigates to the selected target location without needing to be physically pushed.
healthy_conditions: The AGV is in a green state and not currently in a task before the procedure starts.
The Go-to function can be selected for the AGV.
The destination can be selected on the map or by target code.
After confirmation, the AGV moves toward the selected location.
failure_conditions: The AGV is currently in a task.
The AGV cannot be selected or the Go-to function cannot be used.
The AGV does not navigate to the selected target location after confirmation.
The AGV does not pick up a task after being moved and further operator logic is required beyond what the source provides.
escalation_guidance: Do not proceed with this procedure if the AGV is in a task.
Escalate if the AGV does not move to the selected location after confirmation.
Escalate if additional recovery logic is needed after repositioning, because the source only provides a limited recovery example and not a full decision tree.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary visual reference for the Robot Selection Go-to function workflow.

missing_details: The source does not provide a time estimate for completing the Go-to procedure.
The source does not define role boundaries beyond operator-facing actions.
The source does not provide detailed troubleshooting steps if the AGV fails to move after confirmation.
The source does not provide a complete decision process for what to do if the AGV does not pick up a new task after being moved.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required.
